Write a SQL query to get the top 5 customers by total spending.

```sql
SELECT c.id, c.name, SUM(o.amount) AS total_spent
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.name
ORDER BY total_spent DESC
LIMIT 5;
```
This sums each customer's orders and returns the 5 highest. Add a `WHERE o.created_at >= '2026-01-01'` if you want a date range.

Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js app.

A small, production-friendly Dockerfile:

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --omit=dev
COPY . .
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```
Build & run: `docker build -t myapp . && docker run -p 3000:3000 myapp`. Want a multi-stage build to shrink the image further?
